role: Leader of MEK (1979–2003); co-founder of NCRI
bio: Massoud Rajavi joined the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) in 1967 and worked directly with founder [Mohammad Hanifnejad](/people/mohammad-hanifnejad). Arrested and sentenced to death by the Shah's regime, his sentence was commuted to life imprisonment thanks to international pressure led by his brother [Kazem Rajavi](/people/kazem-rajavi). Released in January 1979 during the revolution, he became the MEK's undisputed leader. After the [June 1981 crackdown](/events/june-20-uprising-1981) he fled to Paris with [Abolhassan Banisadr](/people/abolhassan-banisadr), where he co-founded the [National Council of Resistance of Iran](/orgs/national-council-of-resistance-of-iran). Expelled from France in 1986, he relocated to Iraq and established Camp Ashraf under Saddam Hussein's patronage. He launched the disastrous [Operation Forough-e Javidan](/events/operation-forough-e-javidan-1988) in July 1988. He disappeared in March 2003 during the US invasion of Iraq and has not been publicly seen since.
editorial_notes: Status unknown since March 2003. NCRI claims alive in hiding; Iranian state media claimed in 2023 he died under US guard. First wife Ashraf Rabiei killed 1982, second wife Maryam Rajavi.
